content:
Yahoo no longer supports basic authentication (account user name and password) for email client sign-in.

You need to use either oAuth 2.0 (modern prefered method) or setup a 2FA App password.

oAuth 2.0

In Thunderbird, navigate to ... 1. Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server 2. Settings > account > sending mail > outgoung server

a. Change authentication type = oAuth 2.0, then next. b. Click the yahoo "sign-in" button. c. A yahoo sign-in web page will open. d. login and grant Thunderbird the required permissions


App password

AI generated steps.

Web Browser Steps Log into your Yahoo Mail account at mail.yahoo.com. Click your profile icon (top-right), select "Account info," then go to "Account security" on the left. Under "Other ways to sign in," click "Generate and manage app passwords," select "Get started," enter a name like "IMAP Client" or "Outlook," and click "Generate." Copy the 16-character password (shown once) and use it in your email client's password field instead of your regular password
creator: platform34

content: 1. Press the menu button (screen top left) to show the account list. 2. Press the down arrow and select an account. 3. All folders such as Inbox, outbox, trash etc are displayed.
